plugins.name: my-favorite-mcp
plugins.description: Frequently used MCP servers collection

plugins.name: gas-deploy
plugins.description: Google Apps Script deployment management with clasp

plugins.name: broad-review
plugins.description: Custom coding standards code review with local file output

plugins.name: easter-egg
plugins.description: Plant delightful easter eggs in your codebase

plugins.name: parallel-fix
plugins.description: Delegate review feedback fixes to an agent team with file-based task splitting

plugins.name: ai-company-studio
plugins.description: Design and generate AI-only companies as repositories

plugins.name: goal-architect
plugins.description: Help shape a verifiable completion condition for Claude Code's /goal command

plugins.name: pluginize
plugins.description: Watch your work for reusable structure and suggest turning it into a plugin, generated in an isolated background session

plugins.name: handoff-bottle
plugins.description: Bottle up your session state, decisions, and next step so the next session picks up where you left off — a human-readable handoff, not a machine's internal state.

plugins.name: lexicon
plugins.description: Capture the terms you and Claude agree on into a project glossary and replay them into every future session, so a correction made once never has to be made again.

plugins.name: sensei-notebook
plugins.description: Turn the conceptual explanations you were satisfied with into a Q&A notebook, so the next question in that area builds on the answer that already landed instead of starting over.

plugins.name: veto-ledger
plugins.description: Record the directions the user has rejected, and the principle behind each rejection, then replay them into every future session so a declined idea does not return in new clothes.

plugins.name: constitution-gardener
plugins.description: Mine session transcripts for corrections you keep repeating and draft them into principles for your CLAUDE.md — you judge and ratify, the gardener only proposes.

plugins.name: token-bouncer
plugins.description: Watch a session for waste-correlated behavior — repeated agent spawns and the same file read again and again — and quietly warn Claude before the tokens are gone.

plugins.name: dogfood-lab
plugins.description: Log which of your own skills and slash commands actually fire, then report what never did — so the plugin you built but that never triggers stops hiding.

plugins.name: baton
plugins.description: When Claude's work is done but a human still has to verify it on real hardware or in production, emit a copy-pasteable handoff sheet so the baton pass never gets dropped.

plugins.name: mission-control
plugins.description: Post a session's actual outcome to Slack on stop, and brief you on stalled PRs, failing CI, and pending reviews across your repositories.

plugins.name: claude-rpg
plugins.description: Turn your real Claude Code usage stats into an adventurer's log — unlock achievements from your actual sessions, tool calls, and late-night runs.

plugins.name: paste-tube
plugins.description: Capsule pasted content — reports, reviews, error logs from another session — with its source and date, so it survives the conversation and can be carried between sessions.

plugins.name: sketch-first
plugins.description: Before offering concrete proposals for a vague goal, interview the user to draw out the picture in their head and agree on a one-page sketch of what to build.

plugins.name: fleet-sync
plugins.description: Distribute shared conventions, hooks, and settings fragments from an origin repository to a fleet of projects, and detect drift before it silently accumulates.

plugins.name: embassy
plugins.description: Translate an institutional document like your CLAUDE.md into the format and length limits of other AI services, so the same values carry across ChatGPT, Gemini, and generic system prompts.

plugins.name: release-butler
plugins.description: Before a release, inspect whether the code change, the version bump, and the CHANGELOG entry are in sync across the repo and its packages, and report the mismatches — it checks, it does not fix.

plugins.name: adr-keeper
plugins.description: Catch the directional decisions made mid-conversation and record them as Architecture Decision Records, so future sessions read the reasoning instead of quietly reversing it.

plugins.name: omikuji
plugins.description: Draw a daily fortune for your Claude Code work — divined not from randomness but from your own recent usage data, once per day.

plugins.name: idea-forge
plugins.description: Dig your accumulated Claude usage history — command frequency, prompt shapes, usage rhythm, recurring manual work — and propose plugin candidates ranked by evidence, deduped against what you already have installed.

plugins.name: new-toys
plugins.description: Scout Claude Code's latest release notes and translate each new feature into a concrete suggestion for your own setup and plugins.

plugins.name: shosoin
plugins.description: Shelve every document into its proper track — CLAUDE.md, README, docs/, adr/, references/ — inspect each one's content against reality, and reorganize on approval or bootstrap the structure in a new project.

plugins.name: parallel-universe
plugins.description: Spin up several independent attempts at one task in parallel, judge them through different lenses, and graft the winner with the best of the rest.

plugins.name: grimoire
plugins.description: A spellbook of battle-tested Workflow scripts — cast them against a target, inscribe your own, and browse the collected spells.

plugins.name: shikigami
plugins.description: Conjure fenced autonomous routines that run on a schedule when you are away, each bound to report back so its work is never invisible.

plugins.name: tsushinbo
plugins.description: A personal eval harness that issues a report card to your Claude setup — register tasks as subjects, examine them with a fixed grader, and track how scores move when your configuration changes.

plugins.name: tengan
plugins.description: Open the visual-verification loop: capture every visual surface of a project — simulators, web apps, videos, extensions, images — then look at the screenshots and judge them against intent instead of trusting that it works.

plugins.name: kurofune
plugins.description: Call xAI Grok Build from the main Claude conversation like a subagent — MCP tools for task, resume, and doctor; sessions persist so corrections continue the same Grok session.

plugins.name: pixie-dust
plugins.description: Sprinkle life into AI-generated SVG — characters, spinners, icons, blobs, and illustrations alike: asymmetric organic shapes, layered tinted shadows, anticipation, squash and stretch, follow-through, and designed easing, so the output stops looking machine-made.
